title: Piquant Ethiopian Tofu Bake
description: A resinous, springy Ethiopian dish featuring silken tofu, bake, and a hint of spice, perfect for a morning pick-me-up or a weeknight meal.

ingredients.name: niter kibbeh
ingredients.notes: Ethiopian clarified butter or substitute with neutral oil

instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine onion, garlic, ginger, and red bell pepper, and blend until smooth.
In a large skillet, heat the niter kibbeh over medium heat.
Add the blended mixture and cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are softened and the mixture is fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Add the berbere spice mix and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the crumbled tofu and cook, stirring occasionally, until the tofu is coated in the spice mixture and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Sprinkle salt to taste, and garnish with chopped cilantro if desired.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned and the tofu is set.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
